problem.instruction: Read the passage in <A> and the teaching procedure in <B>, and follow the directions.
problem.passage:
Ms. Kim, an English teacher, is selecting lesson objectives to implement into a new lesson. The following are the lesson objectives for reception and production. Lesson Objectives Reception R1. Students can recognize reduced sounds of words. R2. Students can identify specific details from a text or discourse. R3. Students can distinguish between literal and implied meanings. Production P1. Students can explain the sequence of an event in the right order. P2. Students can write a simple journal, letter, or email. P3. Students can argue for and against a topic in a respectful manner. | Step | Teaching Procedure | |------|-------------------| | Step 1 | In groups, students brainstorm the pros and cons of using AI in education and create a mind map. | | Step 2 | Students listen to an audio clip on AI and digital tools in class and complete a worksheet.
Listen to the conversation carefully and follow the directions below.
A. Mark the sentences True or False.
1. Sora says that the use of AI should be prohibited in the classroom. [True/False]
2. Inho asks an AI chatbot to do his assignment. [True/False]
3. Minji compares the outputs on a topic from three different AI chatbots. [True/False]
B. Match the person with his or her concern.
Inho · · Excessive screen time
Minji · · False information
Sora · · Theft of personal data | | Step 3 | Students work together and write rules for the use of digital tools in class, using useful expressions for polite agreement or disagreement.
Class Rules for the Use of Digital Tools
1. e.g., Never download software to a school device without permission. | Identify ONE lesson objective for reception and ONE for production from that are achieved in each step of the teaching procedure in . Then, explain how each objective is achieved with evidence from .

steps.label: 범위 설정 — 학습 목표 6개 확인
steps.explanation:
<A>의 수용(reception) 목표 3개와 표현(production) 목표 3개를 확인한다.
- R1: 축약된 소리 인식 (음성)
- R2: 텍스트·담화에서 specific details 파악 (세부 정보)
- R3: 문자적·함축적 의미 구별 (의미)
- P1: 사건 순서 설명 (순서)
- P2: 일지·편지·이메일 쓰기 (작문)
- P3: 주제에 대해 찬반을 정중하게 argue하기 (토론/쓰기)
<B>의 각 Step 활동 내용과 위 목표 중 하나씩 대응시키는 것이 과제.

steps.label: 이론↔지문 대응 — Step 2 → R2 (Reception)
steps.explanation: Step 2에서 학생들은 오디오 클립을 듣고 워크시트를 완성한다. 워크시트는 (A) T/F 문항과 (B) 인물-걱정사항 매칭으로 구성된다. 이 두 과제 모두 담화(discourse)에서 specific details(특정 세부 정보)를 파악해야 완성 가능하다. R2('identify specific details from a text or discourse')가 정확히 실현되며, R1(음성)·R3(함축)와의 소거로 확정.

steps.label: 이론↔지문 대응 — Step 3 → P3 (Production)
steps.explanation: Step 3에서 학생들은 디지털 도구 사용 규칙을 협력하여 작성한다. 활동 지시문에 'useful expressions for polite agreement or disagreement'가 명시되어 있으며, 이는 주제에 대해 찬반(for and against)을 정중하게 argue하는 P3의 정의와 일치한다. P1(순서 설명)·P2(편지 작문)는 이 활동과 맞지 않으므로 소거.

steps.label: 확정 근거 — 지문 직접 인용
steps.explanation:
R2 근거: Step 2 워크시트 A항목 'Mark the sentences True or False' + B항목 'Match the person with his or her concern' → 담화에서 specific details를 듣고 식별하는 활동.
P3 근거: Step 3 'using useful expressions for polite agreement or disagreement' → 찬반 의견을 정중하게 argue하는 활동 명시.

steps.label: 전략 메모 — 함정 구별
steps.explanation:
Step 1(브레인스토밍+마인드맵)은 찬반 논의 활동이나, 쓰기 결과물이나 정중한 표현 지시가 없어 P3로 보기 어렵다. 핵심은 Step 3의 'polite agreement or disagreement' 지시문이 P3의 'respectful manner'와 직접 대응한다는 점.
Step 2에서 R3(함축 의미 구별) 혼동 주의 — T/F와 매칭은 specific details 파악이지, 함축 의미 구별이 아니다.

key_terms.definition_en: Particular pieces of information in a text or discourse that listeners or readers must identify accurately, as opposed to main ideas or inferred meanings.
key_terms.definition_ko: 텍스트나 담화 속의 특정 세부 정보. 주제나 함축 의미와 달리, 정확하게 파악해야 하는 구체적 사실 정보.
key_terms.exam_usage: R2 목표: Step 2의 T/F 문항과 매칭 과제에서 담화의 specific details 파악 능력 평가

key_terms.definition_en: A production skill in which learners present reasons for and against a position in a structured and respectful way.
key_terms.definition_ko: 학습자가 주제에 대한 찬반 근거를 구조적이고 정중하게 표현하는 표현 기술.
key_terms.exam_usage: P3 목표: Step 3에서 polite agreement/disagreement 표현을 활용하여 디지털 도구 사용 규칙에 대해 찬반 argue

model_answer.essay_content: R2 is achieved in Step 2, where students listen to an audio clip and complete a worksheet by marking statements True or False and matching each person with their concern, identifying specific details from the discourse. P3 is achieved in Step 3, where students collaboratively write class rules for digital tools using useful expressions for polite agreement or disagreement, arguing for and against positions in a respectful manner.

scoring_criteria.criterion: R2 근거: Step 2, T/F + matching으로 specific details 식별 설명
P3 근거: Step 3, 정중한 동의/반대 표현으로 규칙 작성(argue) 설명

variants.label: 답안 A
variants.content: One objective for reception is R2: students identify specific details from the audio clip in Step 2 by marking statements as True or False and matching each person with their concern. One objective for production is P3: in Step 3, students argue for and against the use of digital tools in a respectful manner while discussing and writing class rules with useful expressions for polite agreement or disagreement.

variants.label: 답안 B
variants.content: R2 is applied to Step 2: students listen to the conversation to mark true or false and match each person with their concern. P3 is applied to Step 3: students write rules with useful expressions for polite agreement or disagreement about the use of digital tools.

references.author: Richards, J. C.
references.title: Curriculum Development in Language Teaching
references.excerpt: Language learning objectives are typically framed in terms of skills: reception skills (reading and listening) and production (speaking and writing) skills... Listening objectives may specify that learners can identify main ideas, specific details, or implied meanings from spoken discourse. Speaking objectives may require learners to present opinions, argue a position, or narrate events in sequence.

references.author: Nation, I. S. P. & Newton, J.
references.title: Teaching ESL/EFL Listening and Speaking
references.excerpt: Listening tasks that require identification of specific details — such as true/false judgment or information matching — place demands on learners to process discourse at a fine-grained level, attending to particular facts rather than gist or inferred meaning.

source.concept_explanation:
언어 학습 목표는 수용(reception) 기술과 표현(production) 기술로 구분된다.

Reception 목표:
- R1: 축약 발음 인식 → 음성 지각 능력
- R2: specific details 파악 → T/F·매칭 과제에서 실현
- R3: 함축 의미 구별 → 문자적·비문자적 의미 변별

Production 목표:
- P1: 사건 순서 설명
- P2: 일지·편지·이메일 작성
- P3: 주제에 대해 찬반을 정중하게 argue하기

본 문제 핵심: Step 2의 T/F + 매칭은 담화에서 specific details를 파악하는 R2. Step 3의 polite agreement or disagreement 표현 사용은 P3의 'argue for and against a topic in a respectful manner'와 대응.
